items.reason: site/index.html and site/docs/index.html render as HTML with navigation/styling. OpenAPI pages (site/openapi/*.html) use Redoc per the deterministic check, and the no-raw-doc-links check confirms no .md/.yaml/.yml href under site/.

site/index.html contains explicit 'Platform Provides' and 'App Developer Owns' sections with concrete bullets naming OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI, and Open Badges/CLR; app-owner bullets cover pedagogy, UI quality, and scope minimization.

Each generator (e.g., scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, generate_integration_governance_core.py) reads a single dictionary/*.v1.json and emits SQL comments, OpenAPI JSON, Markdown, and HTML in one run. check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies that descriptions/decision IDs/source standards in artifacts match the dictionary source.

OneRoster, QTI, CASE, Caliper each have dedicated dictionary/*.v1.json with many objects/fields; LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy are combined into dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json which exposes registrations, deployments, launches, AGS/NRPS/DL, OAuth clients, scope policies, sharing rules, consent, retention, and audit objects.

docs/lead-spec-accounting.md enumerates per-spec supported objects/fields and explicit unsupported/deferred ledgers with reasons referencing privacy, scope, and runtime dependencies (e.g., 'Highly sensitive demographic fields are not needed for the current roster/gradebook slice...', 'Delivery requires a tested QTI runtime engine...'). Every dictionary field carries sourceStandard and unsupported entries carry sourceFieldsOrValues lists, enforced by check_dictionary_artifacts.py.

Sampled fields show plain-language descriptions: e.g., person.display_name 'The name shown in user interfaces', caliper_envelope.send_time 'When the sensor says it sent the payload', case_item.full_statement 'The complete text of the standard, competency, skill, objective, or grouping statement'. check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces description coverage across SQL/OpenAPI/Markdown/HTML.

demo/server.js + demo/test.js implement a OneRoster-shaped Express+SQLite slice; demo/test.js asserts `users.users[0].sourcedId === 'USER-ADA'` and that internal IDs aren't exposed on /users. VERIFY.md runs `cd demo && npm run reset-db && npm test`.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py issues live calls to /people, /class_roster, /gradebook_results against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co using the publishable key, returning PostgREST results (not static JSON). The portal documents the curl one-liner.

site/index.html embeds a browser SQL console (sql.js loaded against demo/schema.sql + demo/seed.sql via site/app.js) AND a copy-pasteable curl example against the live Supabase REST URL. Both execute against real data, not screenshots.

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md has 10 numbered decisions covering Person identity, Class/course/section/context, Roles, Enrollment vs membership, Results/scores, Standards alignment, Identifiers, Time/session, Content/resource, and Tenancy. Each entry names specs, conflict, choice, per-spec projection table, and tradeoff in real prose.

standards-overlap-decisions.md contains a machine-readable JSON block (decision-trace markers) listing produces_fields per decision_id, and every dictionary field carries a decision_id. check_dictionary_artifacts.py validates the bidirectional link and fails if a field references an unknown decision or is missing from produces_fields.

supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql adds tenant_id to every fact table, runs `enable row level security` plus `force row level security`, and uses policies like `using (tenant_id = coalesce(nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id', '')::uuid, ...))` — not `true`. tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py creates two Auth users with different app_metadata.tenant_id claims and asserts cross-tenant isolation against the live REST URL; it is wired into VERIFY.md.

dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json defines oauth_scope_policies mapping fully-qualified scopes (e.g., `platform.roster.users.directory:read`, `https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/lti-ags/scope/score`) to exact dictionary field references and a privacy_ceiling. The generated OpenAPI emits a `platformOAuth` security scheme with these scopes and per-field `x-oauthScopes`/`x-privacyClass` metadata.

Every field in the sampled dictionaries carries a non-empty `privacy_class` drawn from a small enumerated vocabulary (`public`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `operational`, `system`, `sensitive`, `privacy_governance`). check_dictionary_artifacts.py requires `privacy_class` as a required field key on every field.

docs/admin-operations.md lists `tenant_rls_test_auth_fixture_setup` and `audit_log_test_auth_fixture_setup` with caller, operation, why_user_jwt_insufficient (specific: 'must create temporary Supabase Auth users with different app_metadata.tenant_id claims'), and last_reviewed 2026-04-26 — within 30 days of the 2026-04-24 research date. The two test files contain matching inline comments referencing those operation IDs; no other service-role usage appears in supabase/functions or scripts.

Migration creates `audit_log` with tenant_id, client_id, scope, purpose, field_accessed, subject_table, subject_id, timestamp, plus a `read_people_sensitive_audited` SECURITY DEFINER function and `audited-roster-read` Edge Function that writes one row per restricted field before returning data. tests/supabase_audit_log_test.py invokes the live URL with a tenant-scoped JWT and verifies rows appear with the required columns; it is in VERIFY.md.

docs/spec-gap-backlog.md includes a 'Rubric Trace For Open Items' table that maps rubric IDs (`tenant_isolation_enforced`, `oauth_scopes_mapped_to_fields`, `audit_log_for_sensitive_reads`, `edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints`, `edge_functions_propagate_user_jwt`, `rls_enabled_on_referenced_tables`, `lead_spec_full_accounting`) to backlog items with status, target slice, and owner notes.

docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md has explicit columns for Structured source, Generated artifacts, Runnable slice, and Unsupported ledger, populated honestly per Lead spec (e.g., QTI 'Runnable slice = No', Caliper = 'Partial: authenticated Edge Function receipt path').

supabase/functions/ contains gradebook-bulk-submit, lti-launch-handler, caliper-event-ingestion, oauth-token-exchange, and audited-roster-read, each with deno.json and an index.ts that constructs the Supabase client from the request Authorization header (no service-role key usage). docs/supabase-hosted-database.md and site/index.html document live URLs and curl examples for each.

All five Edge Function index.ts files initialize `createClient(..., { global: { headers: { Authorization: authorization } } })` from the inbound bearer token; none reference SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. The allowlist in docs/admin-operations.md is required only for the test fixtures, which match its entries.

supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json shows every referenced table (organizations, people, academic_sessions, courses, classes, enrollments, line_items, results, source_identifiers, audit_log) with rowsecurity=true, forceRowSecurity=true, and policies whose `using` clause references `auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id'` — none are `using (true)`. Snapshot generatedAt 2026-04-26 aligns with the latest migration.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py exercises /people, /class_roster, /gradebook_results against the live Supabase project URL using the publishable key from .env.example, hitting PostgREST against a real Postgres backend. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms recent live verification on 2026-04-26.

site/index.html embeds a copy-pasteable curl with the publishable key (`sb_publishable_DaJsnILCWdUIjl4cCaL3Jw_qLy8BPXK`) against the hosted Supabase REST URL plus Edge Function curls; no undocumented env vars are required. The same curl appears in docs/supabase-hosted-database.md.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, and scripts/evaluate_platform.py writes a report containing per-rubric-item status array plus counts (pass/partial/fail/blocked/total) and a `done` flag.

scripts/check_spec_conformance.py still runs in VERIFY.md and writes site/api/spec-conformance.json with `metric: score_gate` and `notACompletionClaim: true`. scripts/codex_loop.py's maybe_publish gates on rubric progress from the LLM evaluator (`rubric_progressed(before_eval, after_eval)`), with the advisory score used only as a fallback signal when the evaluator is unavailable. evaluate_platform.py also reads the advisory report into the LLM context.

scripts/codex_loop.py defines read_overrides(), overrides_paused() (which returns True when a non-comment line equals literal `PAUSE`), checks the pause flag at the top of each iteration before doing any work, and build_prompt injects formatted contents under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md`' section. docs/loop-overrides.md exists with `## Pause`, `## Decisions`, and `## Unblocks` sections.

scripts/codex_loop.py terminates when the LLM evaluator returns `done: True` (`if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): ... return 0`), with hard caps via --max-iterations and a stall_streak guard via rubric_progressed(). No numeric score threshold is used as the termination condition.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
